title: ZXSpace Overview
description: A practical introduction to ZXSpace, its city-defence formation shooter loop, release status, and ZX Series support systems.
summary: ZXSpace is a retro city-defence formation shooter in the ZX Series with alien waves, shields, skins, coins, achievements, and Game Center support.
heroImageAlt: ZXSpace retro space shooter hero artwork

sections.heading: What is ZXSpace?
paragraphs: ZXSpace is part of the [ZX Series](/zx-series), focused on solo city-defence formation shooting, alien waves, shield survival, and score chasing.

The current verified gameplay loop includes touch-and-drag cannon movement, continuous auto-fire, segmented shields, city damage, alien rows, UFO bonus targets, Double Laser and Shield Repair drops, wave coin rewards, unlockable cannon skins, Game Center achievements, leaderboards, and BarkinMad Coins support.

sections.heading: Where to start
paragraphs: Start with How to Play, then read Controls and Game Modes before moving into coins, achievements, leaderboards, and strategy.
Use the guide navigation at the top of each page to move through the standard Website Framework v1.1 structure.

sections.heading: Release notes
paragraphs: The documentation is prepared for release-facing discovery while final App Store delivery and approval details are still being confirmed.
Current central notes confirm the solo city-defence path. Multiplayer behavior may be updated in release notes as the game evolves.

featureShowcases.heading: ZXSpace at a glance
featureShowcases.summary: Overview introduces ZXSpace as a ZX Series space defence arcade game.
body: This screen matters because it gives players the quickest route from discovering the game to understanding how it plays. ZXSpace is presented around space defence runs, so players can see the core loop before reading deeper guides.
Players use this page to decide where to go next: setup, controls, game modes, progression, screenshots, or support topics.
bullets: Links the app landing route to the documentation set.
Keeps public wording focused on player experience and supported features.

image.caption: ZXSpace gameplay artwork introduces the app and its core loop.

featureShowcases.heading: Progression and support surfaces
featureShowcases.summary: ZXSpace documentation connects gameplay with leaderboards, achievements, BarkinMad Coins, tips, FAQ, and screenshots.
body: This matters because ZX Series pages are not only image galleries. They should explain how the player moves from learning the game into repeated play and progress tracking.
Players use these supporting pages after the first session to understand goals, scoring surfaces, unlocks, and practical tips.
bullets: Connects gameplay to progression and support pages.
Keeps route structure consistent across the ZX Series.
Uses existing artwork as placeholders where page-specific screenshots are not available.

image.caption: ZXSpace supporting artwork is used for progression and documentation context.
